scenarios.title: adds a cookie and verifies it appears on the page after refresh @positive
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to the cookies demo page, add a cookie named 'new-cookie-key' via the browser context API, verify the cookie is stored, then click 'Refresh cookies' and confirm the new cookie is visible on the page.
expectedAssertions: context.cookies() returns a cookie named 'new-cookie-key' with value 'new-cookie-value' after addCookies() (secondary API-level assertion)
after clicking 'Refresh cookies', the page visibly displays 'new-cookie-key' in the rendered cookie list (new assertion required — not present in source)

locatorTable.notes: Mechanical By.id → CSS-ID translation per KB §6 Rule 1. Do NOT promote to getByRole without DOM evidence. If reviewer confirms <button> with accessible name 'Refresh cookies', upgrade to page.getByRole('button', { name: /refresh cookies/i }).

locatorTable.original: [new — post-click assertion; no source locator equivalent]
locatorTable.notes: New assertion required by migration-rules.md §2 (every test must end with an observable-outcome assertion). The source ends on an unasserted click. DOM structure of cookies.html is unknown without inspection. See pin 1 and Q3, Q5 in the markdown plan.

hallucinationDefensePins.elementDescription: Cookie display element — new post-click assertion for visible 'new-cookie-key'
hallucinationDefensePins.sourceLocator: [none — new assertion; no DOM locator in source]
hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q5 unresolved: DOM structure of cookies.html not confirmed; assumed cookie name appears as visible text after clicking Refresh.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Open https://bonigarcia.dev/selenium-webdriver-java/cookies.html, click 'Refresh cookies', inspect the element rendering the new cookie name, then replace with the appropriate role/testid locator (e.g. getByRole('cell', { name: 'new-cookie-key' }) if it is a table).
